summary:
Ideal pontic design ensures comfortable and secure contact with minimal tissue irritation for optimal function and patient satisfaction. Given options suggest choice C as most suitable based on these principles. Agent2 reasoned that while specific details about pontic types were lacking, general standards dictate a balance between proper seating against soft tissues and preventing discomfort or damage; thus selecting option C aligns well with this understanding. 

The summary captures both core aspects—comfort/security vs no irritation—and directly connects them back to choosing an answer among provided alternatives.
